reason: Both residual findings are minor. The class attribute omission is compensated by the XML code example which clearly shows the class attribute in use. The missing ThreadContextHandler hint is mitigated because the section content itself contains the term, enabling text-based retrieval to still surface the section.

findings_assessment.justification: The class attribute is visually present in the XML configuration example (`class="nablarch.core.date.BasicBusinessDateProvider"`), so an agent reading s8 would still infer that class is a required attribute and what value to use. The missing explicit prose statement ('class(必須): 使用するBusinessDateProviderの実装クラスを設定する') is redundant given the code example. An agent helping a user configure this component would not produce incorrect guidance from this omission. Phase E appears to have begun fixing s8 but the output was truncated before completion, leaving the prose unchanged.

ThreadContextHandler is absent from s8's hints array despite being mentioned in s8 content. However, the term appears verbatim in the s8 section text ('ThreadContextHandlerによってクリアされる'), so any retrieval system performing text/content matching will still surface s8. Pure hint-based retrieval is the only gap. Furthermore, users asking about ThreadContextHandler's cache-clearing role would typically be directed to the ThreadContextHandler's own documentation first; s8 provides supporting context, not primary information. Phase E left the hints array completely unchanged — no hints were added or modified — indicating the fix step did not address this finding at all.
